title: Douglas MacArthur Address to Congress
rights: public domain (presumed; pre-1963 broadcast, no rights statement on archive.org item)
transcript: Some may say to avoid spread the conflict into an all-out wall with China. Others to avoid Soviet intervention, neither explanation seems valid. For China is already engaging with a maximum power it can commit, And the Soviet will not necessarily mesh its actions with our moves like a cobra. Any new enemy will more likely strike whenever it feels that the relativity and military or other potential is in its favor on a worldwide basis. The tragedy of Korea is further heightened by the fact that as military action is confined to its territorial limits, it condemns that nation, which it is our purpose to save, to suffer the devastating impact of full naval and air bombardment, while the enemy's Sexuaries are fully protected from such attack and devastation. Of the nations of the world, Korea alone up to now is the sole one which has risked its all against communism, the magnificence of the courage and fortitude of the Korean people defies description. they have chosen to risk death rather than slavery. Their last words, to me, were don't scuffle the Pacific. I have just left your fighting sons in Korea. They have not all tests there, and I can report to you without reservation because they They are splendid in every way. It was my constant effort to preserve them, and then the savage conflict honorably and with the least lost of time and a minimum sacrifice of life. This growing bloodshed has caused me the deepest anguish and anxiety. Those gallant men will remain often in my thoughts and in my prayers always. I am closing my 52 years of military service. When I joined the army even before the turn of the century, it was the fulfillment of all all my boyish hopes and dreams. The world has turned over many times since I took the oath on the planet West Point and the hopes and dreams have long since vanished. But I still remember the refrain of one of the most popular baroque ballads of that day, which proclaimed most proudly, that old soldiers never died. They just stayed away. And like the old soldier of that bad. I now close my military career and just fade away, an old soldier who tried to do his duty as God gave in the light to see that duty. Goodbye.
